Off Calendar Feb-07-2020 Off Calendar

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jill Bates-Moore, Esq. of DAHLIA LAW GROUP, T:510.482.2158, for defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT VIVIAN MACK's MOTION For Relief From Default as follows:

Defendant Vivian Mack's motion to vacate the default entered on October 10, 2019 and the default judgment entered on January 16, 2020 is granted. Defendant's motion is timely and she provides an attorney affidavit of fault and proposed pleading. (See Bates-Moore Decl., 1-6.) Relief is therefore mandatory pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, 473(b). The court further notes that plaintiff did not file any meaningful opposition to the motion and does not address section 473(b). Defendant must meet and confer with plaintiff (see Code Civ. Proc. 431.40) and if appropriate, may file the proposed demurrer attached to the moving papers or other responsive pleading by March 2, 2020.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Motion; And Points And Authorities Of It'S Motion To Appear To Court Judgment And Order
Off Calendar Jan-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Vivian L. Mack.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353. Telephone: 415-551-0610. Email: mviscio@sftc.org.

Plaintiff Ronald A. Mack, Sr. was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Vivian L. Mack.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Unpaid Wages and Request for Punitive Damages was filed on November 5, 2108 and personally served on defendant Vivian L. Mack on November 9, 2018.
Statement of Punitive Damages was personally on defendant Vivian L. Mack on February 28, 2019 and filed with the Clerk's Office on October 10, 2019.
Default as to defendant Vivian L. Mack was entered on October 10, 2019.
No DOE defendants were named in plaintiff's complaint.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Ronald A. Mack, and against the defaulted defendant Vivian L. Mack, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $82,320.00.
Punitive Damages - $250,000.00.

Total - $332,320.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
